formulation.label: text_1
formulation.api_concentration_raw: low-dose ibuprofen released from foam dressing

evidence_items.snippet: Persistent venous leg ulcer pain 2.1 Topical ibuprofen (two RCTs, 810 participants) Two RCTs that described the use of ibuprofen as a topical agent in the form of a foam dressing that releases low-dose ibuprofen (Biatain-Ibu (Coloplast A/S)) were included (Arapoglou 2011; Gottrup 2008).
Topical ibuprofen (two RCTs, 810 participants) ... foam dressing that releases low-dose ibuprofen (Biatain-Ibu (Coloplast A/S))

source_notes: Selected blocks indicate a QCM-based thin-film release experiment with ibuprofen release reported as ~2 ug/cm2 after 6 hr. The text route does not explicitly provide diffusion-cell or Franz-cell details, so cell_type/barrier_name are inferred cautiously from the QCM setup and marked uncertain where appropriate.

evidence_items.snippet: A model ibuprofen (IBU) gel formulation was tested by introducing the dosage form through the 3D printed donor compartment and the drug concentration permeated through the skin mimic (silicone membrane) was determined from the 3D printed receptor compartment using UV imaging in real-time.
A 3D printed diffusion cell in similitude of a Franz cell was successfully printed...
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.
SilatosTM silicone membrane of thickness 0.13 mm was purchased...
the silicone membrane of thickness 0.13 mm was placed on a divider between the donor and receptor compartments providing a diffusion area of 3.14 cm2.
The average permeation profile achieved for triplicate run over a 12 h period is shown in Figure 11a.

provenance.route_notes: This is an experimental methods/engineering paper on a diffusion cell platform. Ibuprofen is the model API/formulation, but the main novelty is the 3D printed cell with UV imaging. No clear evidence of a separate formulation table in the provided text.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: abstract, introduction, methods, results, conclusion; repeatedly compared to/defined as being in similitude of a Franz cell and referred to as a Franz cell in figures/captions
route_anchor_evidence.snippet: abstract, introduction, methods, results, conclusion; repeatedly compared to/defined as being in similitude of a Franz cell and referred to as a Franz cell in figures/captions

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Abstract and Methods/Results: ibuprofen permeation through silicone membrane measured in real time over 12 h; absorbance/concentration vs time shown in Figure 11a/b
route_anchor_evidence.snippet: The experiment was conducted for 12 h in triplicate at 37 °C. The absorbance readings at 30 s intervals were extracted from the software. The raw data was processed ... to provide a quantitative assessment of IBU permeated through the membrane.
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.

formulation.label: text_1
formulation.dosage_form: 8 mM aqueous solution of P+-Ibuprofen ion-pair

evidence_items.snippet: We now studied the impact of the UV light induced changes of the P+-azobenzene Ibuprofen ion-pair on permeability. For that, the ion-pair was applied to synthetic model membranes of skin (Strat-M®-membranes).
synthetic model membranes of skin (Strat-M®-membranes).

The trans-isomer had a lag time of about 18 hours and reached equilibrium after 72 hours, whereas the cis-isomer readily penetrated through the model membrane and reached equilibrium in less than 12 hours following exponential patterns (Figure 4C).
the ion-pair was applied to synthetic model membranes of skin

provenance.route_notes: This is an experimental paper focused on photoswitchable ibuprofen ion-pairs and membrane permeation. The permeation study uses synthetic Strat-M membranes, so the best route is IVPT with synthetic membrane rather than skin. No explicit Franz cell method section is present in the supplied text, but the author contributions mention Franz cell assays, confirming use of diffusion-cell-style experiments.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Page 5, author contributions: “Franz cell assays”; no explicit Franz cell figure caption in main text, but the method is named there.
route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Page 5, author contributions: “Franz cell assays”; no explicit Franz cell figure caption in main text, but the method is named there.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Page 5, Figure 4C and adjacent text: permeation of Ibuprofen ion pairs through membranes; trans-isomer lag time ~18 h and equilibrium after 72 h, cis-isomer equilibrium in <12 h.
route_anchor_evidence.snippet: “The trans-isomer had a lag time of about 18 hours and reached equilibrium after 72 hours, whereas the cis-isomer readily penetrated through the model membrane and reached equilibrium in less than 12 hours…”

“We now integrated Ibuprofen ... into azobenzene ion-pairs… The triethylammonium Ibuprofenate salt (N+-Ibuprofen; ... ) being liquid at room temperature ... and the trimethylphosphonium Ibuprofenate (P+-Ibuprofen) being a crystalline solid…”

formulation.label: text_1

evidence_items.snippet: IBU (1%)
Preparation of the LBG/Xanthan hydrogel loaded with the vesicles.
even after 48 h, the release was not complete and only about 60% of the loaded drug was capable to diffuse from the matrix

provenance.route_notes: This is a topical formulation characterization and in vitro release study, not a skin permeation/penetration study. No diffusion cell or Franz cell is mentioned in the supplied text. Ibuprofen is explicitly included as a model drug, and the release endpoint is presented in figures/tables within the main text.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Release profiles and related release percentages are reported in the Results/Discussion, with time-based release curves for CALC, NR, CAFF, and IBU (Figures 2 and 3; Tables 1 and 2).

A new formulation consisting of non ionic surfactant vesicular structures, niosomes (NSVs), loaded with model molecules calcein (CALC), nile red (NR), ibuprofen (IBU) or caffeine (CAFF), and embedded in a hydrogel matrix... the system locust bean gum/xanthan (1:1), prepared at 60 °C, was used to entrap the vesicles (Tween 20/cholesterol 1:1)...

evidence_items.snippet: The in-vitro dissolution results showed that there was release of drug up to 168 h, i.e., 7 days after single application of gel into the dissolution medium.
At the end of 168 h, 96.34% of moxifloxacin and 95.45% of ibuprofen were released into the medium.
In SRP + LDD group, along with SRP, LDD of 0.1 ml of moxifloxacin and ibuprofen gel was injected...
release of drug... into the dissolution medium

provenance.route_notes: Ibuprofen is explicitly part of the gel formulation. No diffusion cell or Franz cell is mentioned in the supplied text. This is a primary clinical + in-vitro dissolution study on chronic periodontitis with split-mouth design and local drug delivery.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Plaque index (PI), gingival index (GI), probing depths (PD) and clinical attachment level (CAL) were recorded at baseline and 1 and 3 months after the treatment... The in-vitro dissolution results showed that there was release of drug up to 168 h, i.e., 7 days after single application of gel into the dissolution medium.
It was prepared by using 50 mg of moxifloxacin and 50 mg of ibuprofen in 750 mg of polycaprolactone gel (biodegradable) by using 188.67 μl of dichloromethane as solvent.
